name: Priego to Cañamares via Estrecho de Priego Trail
name_original: Cañamares desde Priego — ida y vuelta por el Estrecho de Priego
description: Cycle 11.5 miles (18.5 km) through the Serranía de Cuenca on this moderate touring route, passing the Estrecho de Priego.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Priego de Córdoba are set within the dramatic landscapes of the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park, a UNESCO Geopark. The region features rugged limestone mountains, deep valleys, and extensive olive groves, creating a varied backdrop for cycling. A significant feature is the Vía Verde del Aceite, an old railway line transformed into a 128-kilometer natural path. This area offers a network of routes suitable for different cycling preferences, from gentle greenways to challenging climbs. Best touring cycling routes around Priego The most popular touring cycling route is Cañamares from Priego — round trip through the Estrecho de Priego, a 11.5 miles (18.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 12 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate challenge with varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Villaconejos de Trabaque y Albalate de las Noqueras por la Hoz del Conde — circular desde Cañamares, a difficult 23.9 miles (38.5 km) path. This route provides significant elevation gain and scenic views of the surrounding natural park. Local touring cyclists also love the Fuertescusa from Cañamares — round trip through the Puerta del Infierno, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 46 minutes. Touring cycling around Priego is defined by limestone mountains, extensive olive groves, and the accessible Vía Verde del Aceite. The network of routes offers options for easy rides, moderate challenges, and difficult climbs, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Priego are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 40 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Priego's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Priego?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes around Priego de Córdoba, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult paths to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Priego?
A: The terrain around Priego de Córdoba is quite varied, dominated by the dramatic landscapes of the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park. You can expect rugged limestone mountains, deep valleys, and extensive olive groves. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1093937669/canamares-from-priego-round-trip-through-the-estrecho-de-priego" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañamares from Priego — round trip through the Estrecho de Priego&lt;/a&gt;, offer varied terrain, while others feature significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Priego?
A: Yes, there are easy touring cycling routes available. A notable option is the Vía Verde del Aceite, a 128-kilometer natural path following an old railway line, making it safe, signposted, and accessible for cyclists of all levels, including families. For a shorter, easy option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1093963639/fuertescusa-from-canamares-round-trip-through-the-puerta-del-infierno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuertescusa from Cañamares — round trip through the Puerta del Infierno&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical landmarks. You can encounter impressive geological formations within the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park, including poljes and lapiaz. Routes often pass through picturesque olive groves. Notable natural features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5404032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañamares natural pool&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5650912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta del Infierno&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5412048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz del Conde gorge&lt;/a&gt;. You might also spot the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5404050" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Hocino Gorge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for advanced riders?
A: Absolutely. Priego de Córdoba offers several challenging routes with significant elevation gains, often featured in professional cycling races like La Vuelta a España. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1364026809/villaconejos-de-trabaque-y-albalate-de-las-noqueras-por-la-hoz-del-conde-circular-desde-canamares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaconejos de Trabaque y Albalate de las Noqueras por la Hoz del Conde — circular desde Cañamares&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 38.5 km path with substantial climbing, providing scenic views of the natural park.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through charming villages?
A: Yes, many routes offer the opportunity to visit charming white villages such as Priego de Córdoba itself, Zuheros, and Carcabuey. These towns often feature historic castles, like Priego de Córdoba Castle, and viewpoints such as the Mirador del Adarve, allowing cyclists to immerse themselves in Andalusian culture and cuisine.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about touring cycling in Priego?
A: The touring cycling routes in Priego are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the stunning natural landscapes of the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park, and the accessibility of routes like the Vía Verde del Aceite.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular touring cycling routes around Priego de Córdoba. These loops allow you to start and end your ride in the same location, offering convenience. An example of a challenging circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1364026809/villaconejos-de-trabaque-y-albalate-de-las-noqueras-por-la-hoz-del-conde-circular-desde-canamares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaconejos de Trabaque y Albalate de las Noqueras por la Hoz del Conde — circular desde Cañamares&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is the Vía Verde del Aceite suitable for touring cyclists?
A: Absolutely. The Vía Verde del Aceite is an excellent choice for touring cyclists. This 128-kilometer natural path follows an old railway line, offering a safe, signposted, and relatively flat route. It features 13 splendid metallic viaducts and 3 tunnels, with many refurbished stations providing tourist services like bars and restaurants. You can learn more about it on &lt;a href="https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/V%C3%ADa_verde_del_Aceite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wikipedia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the options for parking near touring cycling routes in Priego?
A: While specific parking areas for each route aren't detailed, the charming white villages in the region, such as Priego de Córdoba itself, often provide parking facilities. Many routes, especially those starting from or passing through these towns, would have accessible parking nearby. It's advisable to check local parking options in the starting village of your chosen route.

Q: Are there cycling-friendly accommodations or services in the area?
A: Yes, Priego de Córdoba is becoming increasingly popular with cyclists, and you can find specialized boutique hotels that cater to their needs. These accommodations often provide services like mapped routes and GPX tracks, making it easier to plan your touring cycling adventures. Many villages along the routes also offer cafes and restaurants for refreshments.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling in Priego de Córdoba?
A: The region experiences a Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for touring cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. These seasons provide ideal weather for enjoying the scenic routes and exploring the natural park.
tips: The Hoz de Conde formed by the erosion of the Escabas river, is an impressive landscape, very close to the town of Priego.

A beautiful place for all nature lovers. It is a canyon that we can cross by road, one of the most picturesque in Spain. Enjoying the views do not forget to be careful going through the curves next to the rock walls of up to 400 m. Luckily there is little traffic.

Wonderful place that you can visit at any time of the year. It is usually more crowded in the summer months since the beach located here is an ideal place to enjoy a refreshing bath in its waters. It also has parking from where you can start your routes.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Cañamares from Priego cycling route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route is approximately 18.5 km long and generally takes around 1 hour and 12 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your cycling pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as moderate and requires good fitness. While it's suitable for various skill levels, the approximately 250 meters of elevation gain and descent suggest there might be some challenging ascents for beginners.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: You can expect mostly paved surfaces, which is ideal for touring bicycles. The route winds through the impressive landscapes of the Serranía de Cuenca, offering picturesque views of rock formations and cliffs carved by rivers.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Cañamares from Priego cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Priego. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Priego serves as a gateway to the area, and you should be able to find parking within the town to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What natural features or landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning natural beauty. You'll pass through the scenic Estrecho de Priego, a notable canyon, and the Hoz del Conde gorge. You'll also find the Cañamares natural pool, a beautiful lake feature.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given its location in the Serranía de Cuenca, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful natural colors. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring colder weather, though the paved surfaces make it accessible year-round.

faqs.question: Is the Cañamares from Priego cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily for cycling on paved surfaces, dogs are generally welcome in the natural areas of Serranía de Cuenca. However, always keep your dog on a leash, especially when encountering other cyclists or wildlife, and ensure you clean up after them.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this cycling route or the Serranía de Cuenca park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or to access the general natural areas of the Serranía de Cuenca. It is freely accessible for outdoor activities.

faqs.question: Does this cycling route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with sections of other trails. You will briefly cross parts of the Sendero de la Degollada, the Ruta del Mirador del Rodenal, and the Camino Convento.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Priego, a town that serves as a hub for the region. You'll find various amenities there, including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options to refresh yourself before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 'Cañamares from Priego — round trip through the Estrecho de Priego' is designed as a round trip, meaning it's a loop trail that brings you back to your starting point in Priego.
